parsing_res_list.block_content: Executive Order No.13166: Improving Access to Services for Persons with  Limited English Proficiency (LEP)
August 11, 2000: President Bill Clinton issued the Executive Order 13166.
Designed to improve access to f federally conducted and federally assisted programs/activities for LEP persons
Federal agencies were asked to develop and implement ta system to provide LEP persons access to services.

Each federal agency was asked to prepare a plan to improve 
access to LEP persons, and s submit plans to the U.S.
Department of Justice.
Executive eOrdercharged the Department of Justice with the responsibility of providing LEP guidance to federal agencies.

core_content.markdown:
# Executive Order No.13166: Improving Access to Services for Persons with  Limited English Proficiency (LEP)

August 11, 2000: President Bill Clinton issued the Executive Order 13166.



Designed to improve access to f federally conducted and federally assisted programs/activities for LEP persons 

Federal agencies were asked to develop and implement ta system to provide LEP persons access to services.

Each federal agency was asked to prepare a plan to improve 

access to LEP persons, and s submit plans to the U.S.

Department of Justice.



Executive eOrdercharged the Department of Justice with the responsibility of providing LEP guidance to federal agencies.
